title: veronica dissecta subsp. lanuginosa
intro: Veronica dissecta subsp. lanuginosa is a subspecies of the Veronica dissecta plant, commonly known as woolly speedwell, which is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Veronica dissecta subsp. lanuginosa include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and act as a natural antacid, making it a popular herbal remedy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
